rulings.Case Title: THOMAS SANCHEZ VS. THE LEO EPP COMPANY, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, December 24, 2009, line 2, DEFENDANT KIE 1740 INVESTORS, LLC, RKI 1740 INVESTORS, LLC, BJI 1740 INVESTORS, LLC, BJI 1740 IRR INVESTORS, LLC, LIG 1740 INVESTRORS, LLC, LIG 1740 IRR INVESTORS, LLC MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Motion for summary judgment denied as to "subsequent owner defendants." Trible issue of fact raised regarding when they became owners of the property. Tenants in common contract signed 8/29/07 indicates the entities had a ownership interest before 10/18/07. Triable issue of fact whether the ownership interest occurred on 8/29/07 or long before that time. CC sec. 1466 not applicable. Defendant shifted the burden on the other issues under Andrews vs. Foster Wheeler (2006) 138 Cal.App. 4th 96 relying on plaintiff's factually devoid special interrogatory responses. Summmary adjudication granted as to concealment, intentional infliction of emotional distress, and punitive claims. No evidence that defendants had actual knowledge of the mold hazard. Summary adjudication denied as to wrongful eviction and negligent misrepresentation. Triable issues of fact whether defendants should have known of mold hazard, and whether defendants negligently misrepresented the condition of the units. Triable issue of fact whether landlord diligently inspected the premises for defects and properly acted to corrrect those defects. =(302/CWW)
